source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/dot-slides-after-delay-in-polkadot-etf/

data.title: Proposed GENIUS Act Targets Stablecoin Issuance and Tech Companies
data.description:
According to PANews, a draft of the GENIUS Act has been revealed, outlining several key regulatory measures for stablecoin issuers. The draft aims to prohibit misleading claims of FDIC insurance by stablecoin issuers and the use of terms associated with the U.S. government in their naming conventions. Additionally, it seeks to prevent non-financial tech companies, such as Meta and Google, from issuing stablecoins, reinforcing the separation between banking and commerce.

The proposed legislation also aims to enhance enforcement capabilities against violations and expand ethical oversight for special government employees, including figures like Elon Musk. These measures are part of a broader effort to regulate the stablecoin market and ensure transparency and accountability among issuers and related entities.

data.title: Sonic Labs will not renew its contract with market maker Wintermute
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Sonic Labs strategy director assistant.sonic announced on the X platform that it would not renew the contract with market maker Wintermute, ending the five-year exclusive partnership between the two parties. The Sonic team will turn to market makers that can provide "MM++" services, and expects new partners to not only provide centralized trading platform support, but also actively participate in the construction of the DeFi ecosystem and support application growth.

data.title: Starknet Teams Up with Lombard to Add Bitcoin Liquid Staking to DeFi
data.description:
TL;DR Starknet added LBTC to its network, a 1:1 Bitcoin-backed token created by Lombard Finance for liquid staking. The integration lets users access DeFi applications using Bitcoin without moving funds from its original blockchain, expanding available tools. The network was also recognized as a Stage 1 ZK Rollup, reinforcing its security and decentralization within the. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/starknet-teams-up-with-lombard-to-add-bitcoin-liquid-staking-to-defi/

data.title: He Yi introduces the Binance Sprout Program: Open to the world, encouraging applicants from diverse backgrounds to join
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance co-founder He Yi introduced the latest talent recruitment project "New Sprout Program" in the AMA, which will find and cultivate talents who are passionate about the blockchain and cryptocurrency industry. He Yi said that Binance not only pays attention to the technical background of candidates, but also attaches importance to innovative thinking, rapid learning ability, enthusiasm for industry development and desire for success. The "New Sprout Program" is open to applications from all over the world and is suitable for young people from different backgrounds and development stages. Applicants from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to join and jointly promote the development of the blockchain ecosystem.

data.title: Bitcoin Predicted to Outperform Gold in Late 2025, Analysts Say
data.description: According to PANews, analysts at JPMorgan have indicated that Bitcoin may continue to outperform gold in the latter half of 2025, driven by weakening gold prices and shifts in capital flows. The report highlights that companies like MicroStrategy are consistently purchasing Bitcoin, and several U.S. states, including New Hampshire, are incorporating Bitcoin into their strategic reserves, allowing up to 5% asset allocation in the cryptocurrency. Additionally, the maturation of the crypto derivatives market and clearer regulatory frameworks are attracting institutional participation.

data.title: Sonic Labs Secures Court Order to Wind Down Multichain Foundation
data.description:
TL;DR The Singapore High Court has authorized Sonic Labs to force the liquidation of Multichain Foundation following the 2023 hack that caused over $210 million in losses. Sonic Labs, formerly known as the Fantom Foundation, accused Multichain of non-cooperation, prompting the legal action. Accounting firm KPMG has been appointed as the official liquidator to oversee. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/sonic-labs-secures-court-order-to-wind-down-multichain-foundation/

data.title: AML Bot's Vulnerabilities Exploited by Criminal Organizations, Reports Suggest
data.description:
According to PANews, ZachXBT has revealed that the anti-money laundering tool AML Bot has been exploited by criminal organizations to evade address tracking, citing weak internal controls as the source of vulnerabilities. Examples include the 2024 Genesis creditor theft case, where attackers used AML Bot to bypass asset freezes, and the 2025 ransomware group BlackBasta recommending the tool for address verification. Additionally, Krebs' blog disclosed that its clients include dark web tool Antinalysis.

Previously, blockchain forensics company AMLBot released a report highlighting delays in Tether's freezing mechanism, which has resulted in over $78 million in stolen assets.

data.title: CoreWeave Reports Significant Revenue Growth in First Quarter
data.description:
According to Foresight News, CoreWeave has released its first financial report since going public, revealing a substantial 420% increase in revenue for the first quarter ending March 31, compared to the same period last year, when it earned $188.7 million. During this quarter, CoreWeave entered into a five-year agreement with OpenAI, valued at up to $11.9 billion.

Additionally, Microsoft accounted for 62% of CoreWeave's revenue in 2024. CoreWeave CEO Mike Intrator, in an interview with CNBC, disclosed that beyond the initial five-year agreement, OpenAI signed an additional $4 billion collaboration deal with CoreWeave after the first quarter ended. This agreement involves CoreWeave providing cloud computing capabilities to OpenAI.

data.title: CoreWeave Secures $4 Billion Cloud Computing Deal with OpenAI
data.description: According to Odaily, CoreWeave has announced a new agreement with OpenAI, valued at $4 billion, to supply cloud computing capabilities to the AI research organization. This collaboration aims to enhance OpenAI's computational resources, supporting its ongoing projects and developments in artificial intelligence.

data.title: Aethir announces Checker Nodes buyback program, which will be officially launched on May 22
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Decentralized cloud computing protocol Aethir announced that it will launch the Checker Node NFT repurchase plan on May 22, 2025, allowing node holders to sell their Checker Node NFT back to the Aethir Foundation at an anchor price. This initiative aims to provide Checker Node holders with greater flexibility while enhancing the stability and sustainability of the Aethir overall network ecosystem. All Checker Node holders can participate to ensure that every user can use the exit option fairly. After the NFT is sold back, the relevant entrustment relationship will be automatically terminated.
It is reported that the initial repurchase pool of the Checker Node repurchase plan is 200 million ATHs. If user demand is higher than expected, additional funds will be added depending on the situation. The repurchase-related contracts have passed strict audits and have built-in fault protection mechanisms to reduce risks caused by code vulnerabilities or operational errors.

data.title: 0x acquires competitor Flood to compete for DEX aggregation market share
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Decentralized trading infrastructure company 0x announced the acquisition of competitor Flood, aiming to enhance its competitiveness in the decentralized exchange (DEX) aggregation market. The specific acquisition amount has not been disclosed. This acquisition is the first merger and acquisition of 0x since its establishment in 2017, and it mainly focuses on Flood's proprietary aggregation software and development team. 0x CEO Amir Bandeali said that Flood's technology performed well in transaction simulation tests, helping to improve transaction efficiency and compete for market share. Currently, the market value of DEX aggregators is about US$2.3 billion, 1inch and CoW Swap dominate on chains such as Ethereum, while Jupiter leads on Solana. (CoinDesk)
